channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who express their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Australia to hold general elections on May 3 with inflation and a housing shortage major issues
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australians will go to the polls on May 3 for general elections with high costs of living and a shortage of housing likely weighing against the government as Prime Minister Anthony Albanese 's center-left Labor Party seeks a second three-year term.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge says heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional as states face mounting lawsuits
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A federal judge's rebuke of Texas housing prisoners in lockups without air conditioning stopped short of ordering a fix before summer in what has become of the country's biggest lawsuits over keeping prisoners safe during dangerous extreme heat. But U.S.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How this auto union leader's support for Trump's tariffs scrambled labor politics again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has once again scrambled the politics of organized labor and the working class with his planned tariffs on auto imports.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The woman who killed Tejano music icon Selena in 1995 has been denied parole
item.description: The woman who has served nearly three decades in prison for killing Tejano music legend Selena Quintanilla-Pérez, has been denied parole by a Texas board.
item.dc:creator: Juan A. Lozano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What we know about the deadly air crash between a passenger jet and a US Army helicopter
item.description: A congressional hearing Thursday reinforced the idea that the January midair collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter that killed all 67 people aboard both aircraft could have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione may get a laptop in jail to work on his defense in CEO murder case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge cleared the way Thursday for Luigi Mangione to get a laptop behind bars — if his jailers agree — so he can examine documents and other material in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First rain and then fire chase people from their homes in North and South Carolina
item.description: At least a half-dozen large wildfires continue to burn in the Blue Ridge Mountains of South Carolina and North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins And Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: As many as 6 hurricanes forecast to strike US this season
item.description: “It only takes one landfall to create a devastating season,” AccuWeather chief meteorologist Jonathan Porter said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Civil rights groups say push to dismantle Education Department will undo hard-won gains
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The rights of Americans to self-determine how to educate their children — a hotly contested matter that stretches back to at least the Civil War — have long been intertwined with the principle of equally and equitably educating children across racial lines.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says at least 300 foreign students' visas have been revoked
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Thursday at least 300 foreign students have had their visas revoked amid the Trump administration's immigration agenda, a far higher number than was previously known.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KTLA 5

item.title: Violin thief-turned-bank robber sentenced to 4 years in prison
item.description: A California man convicted of orchestrating a scheme to steal valuable violins, and then robbing a bank when he realized federal investigators were closing in on him, has been sentenced.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Illegal immigrant nabbed by Georgia police after allegedly preying on four children
item.description: An illegal immigrant from Guatemala was arrested in Georgia after allegedly molesting four children under the age of 14, according to the Valdosta Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bryan Kohberger trial: Idaho student murders survivor draws mask intruder wore on night of stabbings
item.description: An Idaho student murders survivor drew a sketch of the mask she says she saw on an intruder in her house the night four of her friends were killed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tostitos chips sold in 13 states recalled over undeclared allergen
item.description: Frito-Lay issued the recall Wednesday, after it was discovered that some bags of Tostitos contained the wrong product.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Congress questions the FAA, US Army and NTSB over deadly midair collision in DC
item.description: The head of the National Transportation Safety Board appeared alongside officials from the Federal Aviation Administration and the Army's aviation division at a hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: DC-bound Amtrak passenger may have exposed travelers to measles, health officials say
item.description: The passenger rode an Amtrak train in the Northeast last week before visiting an urgent care center in DC, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: University of Alabama doctoral student arrested by ICE
item.description: The University of Alabama says that one of its doctoral students has been arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Watch live: Senate holds hearing on rising antisemitism on college campuses
item.description: The Senate Health, Education, Labor and Pensions Committee is holding a hearing on the perceived rise of antisemitism on college campuses.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist submarine sinks near Egypt, leaving 6 dead and 9 inured, officials say
item.description: The submarine, which was sailing off one of the beaches in the tourist promenade area, had carried 45 tourists of various nationalities.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Honorary Secret Service agent DJ Daniel tours training facility, meets top officials
item.description: Trump-honored DJ Daniel, the 13-year-old cancer survivor made honorary Secret Service member, reported for duty at the James J. Rowley Training Center in Beltsville, Maryland, last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge says extreme heat in Texas prisons is unconstitutional but doesn't order they install AC
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional,” but declined to order the state to immediately start installing air conditioning, which could cost billions.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Which fast-food burger reigns supreme? A recent survey reveals consumers' favorite one
item.description: Fast-food fans have spoken and have determined that In-N-Out Burger has the best fast-food burger, according to a recent Delish article. The food publication asked fans on social media which fast-food burger reigns supreme, with many claiming In-N-Out takes the top spot.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: A heart-shaped note was found in socks bound for Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Someone tucked a heart-shaped note of encouragement into socks packed for Luigi Mangione to wear to court last month in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case, according to court documents made public Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Stepmother accused of holding Connecticut man captive for over 20 years appears in court
item.description: Kimberly Sullivan arrived at Waterbury Superior Court just before 10 a.m., where she was expected to plead not guilty to kidnapping and assault charges, according to her lawyers. But the case was continued until Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements to buy them in kits online.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: Perpetual adoration is a growing Catholic trend. A Holy Year event is scheduled for the weekend
item.description: HIALEAH, Fla. (AP) — Twice a week, Luisa Arguello and her husband spend from 2 a.m. to 3 a.m. praying in the perpetual adoration chapel at St. Benedict Catholic Church, tucked on a quiet palm-fringed residential street in the Miami suburb of Hialeah. “Your body clock gets used to it.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance to accompany wife to Greenland amid backlash
item.description: Vance’s announcement adds another layer of tension to an already fraught atmosphere surrounding his wife’s planned visit.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The MLB teams with the highest
item.description: A California team has the highest median ticket price, a new report found, but they don't play in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump admin from deporting South Korean Columbia student
item.description: A federal judge Tuesday blocked the Trump administration from deporting a Columbia University student who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations at the school.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Transgender Americans aim to block Trump's passport policy change
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When Ash Lazarus Orr went to renew his passport in early January, the transgender organizer figured it would be relatively routine. But more than two months on, Orr is waiting to get a new passport with a name change and a sex designation reflecting who he is.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More states move to ban cellphones in school as Georgia is latest to act
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — More states are moving to ban or restrict cellphones in school, a trend that continues to win bipartisan support in legislatures.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illinois returns stolen land to Prairie Band Potawatomi Nation
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The Prairie Land Potawatomi Nation has reclaimed land in Illinois that was promised to the tribe's leader 175 years ago but stolen by the federal government 20 years later. A law signed by Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump officials' war planning group chat breach
item.description: Washington was rocked Monday by a truly extraordinary story. The editor in chief of The Atlantic, Jeffrey Goldberg, recounted how he had — apparently inadvertently — been added to a group chat featuring some of the most senior members of the United States government.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9
